content: I started using this app back in 2019, when it was still called Moodpath. This app helped me understand my symptoms and patterns, making it easier to navigate my mental health day to day. I used the notes from the app to guide discussions, and my doctor even said it made working with me easier. A simple but powerful tool that truly supports professional care.

Highly recommended!

content: I really like this app. Its great for mood tracking and helpful in practicing self-awareness/recognizing your patterns and behaviors. If you are on top of logging into the app every morning, noon, and night for like a week or two, you'll get your results so fast and even after you get results they still track for more, I also like the many varietal questions, some are a bit personal but thats what makes the app real. I think that there should be more check in emotions like fear or jealousy
replyContent: Your feedback is very important to us! It's great that you find the app helpful for mood tracking and self-awareness. Your suggestion for more on emotions such as fear and jealousy is noted and we will discuss this with our team. Thank you for helping us to continuously improve the MindDoc app. Kind regards, your MindDoc team.

content: No unsolicited, unwanted, unwarranted advice from people who don't even know you. It's great.
replyContent: Dear Kirstin, thank you for your positive feedback. For further suggestions on how to improve, feel free to contact us via feedback@minddoc.de. Best regards, Your MindDoc Team.

content: It's a really good app. It has an easy database and stores questions and has them relatively easy to answer. I redownloaded the app as I joticied my mental health was going. I like to take like five minutes each question period to do that and some relaxing thing to just.. ground myself.
replyContent: Hi Morgan, thank you for sharing your experience. We are glad to hear that your enjoy the app and that you find it useful to ground yourself. This kind of feedback is a great motivation to us. Best regards, your MindDoc Team.

Good for realizing yourself

There is a lot more I could say, but those are the biggest positives to me.
absolutely useless. just a fancy mood tracker.

I really enjoy getting new questions to answer all the time.
replyContent: Thank you for your feedback, this does not sound intentional at all. Please contact us via feedback@minddoc.de so that we can find out what could be wrong. Best regards, your MindDoc-Team

content: Easy Simple convenient

Your app does not take disabled people into account and they disproportionately suffer from mental illness. Many questions are geared towards work, which I am unable to do. I've also been asked if I'm afraid of becoming seriously ill. I'm already seriously ill. You assume that everyone using this app is physically well and able to work. It is erasure of disabled people. Please consider consulting with people from the disabled community on how to include us.
replyContent: thank you so much for reaching out about this. We received your feedback and will discuss this thoroughly in our team to try and find ways and solutions to make every MindDoc-User feel included and seen. Incase you have specific suggestions on how we can improve, please contact us via feedback@minddoc.de. Best wishes, your MindDoc

content: This has honestly helped me want to achieve my objectives and be better I appreciate this app so much I needed it...

I highly recommend this app. If you want an app that you can log in constitenly to check your mental well-being at not cost, this is perfect for you. It asks relevant questions based on what you've answered. What's got me annoyed, though, is that the insights aren't free. How am I supposed to get better if I can't read the insight? These app creators keep forgetting that some of us are in college or are underage and don't have the financials to pay. But overall, it's a good app.

I didn't expect this app to be as good as it is. It's helped me navigate through the aftermath of my panic disorder and gave me helpful tools for the future. The questions being asked 3 times a day can be overwhelming at times, but what I've found is that if you're busy or need to take a break from it, the app recognizes that and stops sending notifications after being ignored for a few days. I ended up buying the annual subscription and have recommended it to many of my friends and loved ones!
I like the questions throughout the day. It makes me stop and recenter to see how I'm feeling.

I'm really trying to use the app to cope with losing my mental state after losing my pregnancy at 7 months almost dying and having a hysterectomy at 32.. I'm finding it satisfying in a way nothing else did. I didnt want to do support groups and this was a good alternative.
It's an absolutely amazing app! The results are extremely accurate, and they provide very helpful insights! I hope to see more in the app in the future, however.
This app is helping me discover things about myself that I didn't know about and also giving me solutions on how to tackle some of the negative things.
very useful app, would suggest it to everyone, beautiful, easy to use and totally insightful
I really enjoy the app, but my daily questions only shows pictures so it's hard to understand, I have to figure it out apart from that I love the app♥️

content: When it works as intended, very useful! However it often does not actually record entries, especially on stressful/bad days, and then gives you the message that you need more entries for a more accurate result... I've even uninstalled and redownload and the issue does not fix. Worse, it sometimes effects later entries and thus your day looks better/worse than it actually was. This issue has been off and on over the years of me having this app, but has gotten far worse recently..

It is a very good app, and one course per month and daily question are very good features, but if you want to access full courses, you have to purchase subscription. But unfortunately subscription model is not friendly with indian market. But anyway, thanks.

This app is exactly what I needed to help me with my anxiety and depression. The app asks questions 3 times a day and tracks your mood. It then gives you insights on how to make situations better. But this app is so amazing and insightful that I believe that this is all I need. This is a must have!

Simple and effective. You can tell that the developers put a lot of effort into this app. The only thing I can think of that could be improved is that the questions are getting a bit repetitive. Adding a few more would be great. Overall an amazing app :)

I love the app and questions they ask. It really help with the assessment you get every 14 days. It shows you were you are struggling. A lot of different topics you can read about. I told several people about it.

content: So far so good it does more than any other app I've tried only thing is, I really need help and this is so expensive!! Mental health help should not be expensive

Take your mental health serious. Please. Great . I find this really useful .

This is no longer a free app. It doesn't function as a mood tracker unless you pay $50. It'll give you a couple months free, then hold your mood results ransom. This is exploitative and scammy. I gave so much to this app/company. I've been using it for years (when it was called moodpath). I participated in their clinical study when given the option. I got my friends to use the app; recommended it to therapists. I'll warn them against it now. It's ransomware that preys on the mentally vulnerable.
replyContent: Hello, our team would like to make MindDoc accessible to as many people as possible which is why we offer as much of the core content of the app as possible free of charge. However, as the app grows more complex we have to find ways to balance out the work. This is why we are offering a freemium model. Best wishes, your MindDoc team

content: This is the first free mood tracking app I've used and it helps me do just that. In the beginning I was delighted by the light design of the app and insightful suggestions based on my answers. Now after 3 months it is starting to be a bit tiring to answer the same, sometimes vague questions 3 times a day. I mean the app asks if something has happened "ever" , "lately" or "in general" - 3 times a day! It also doesn't recognise stress-based problems, which I hope will be added in the future.

Cannot recommend this app enough, it has changed the way I reflect on my experiences and helps me find correlations in my behaviour and mood, provides a historical look at how you've been and where you've come from. I'm giving it 4 stars for two reasons, a) the question pool it asks you from has gotten repetitive after 3 months and it feels like it will ask you those same questions almost every day, and b) you can't add extra positive and negative emotions, you're stuck with very limited choices

I'm on the free version as it is expensive to subscribe, which is why I've marked down 1 star. I can't afford it at the moment which is a shame as the additional content does look good, but personally I cannot afford the cost. However, the free version has been fab so far, with some really handy insights, good reminders to log and track feelings, and help towards getting into the habit of doing this, to try and help me out of a really really dark place.
Wish this app was free all around. I dont have money for the paid stuff.👎

content: Really easy to use and asks different questions 3 times a day, making it easy for you to understanding how you are feeling and to track your moods easily through the week. First, there can be a darkmode option and the second is at the end of the questions the mood selection screen which can be a drop-down multiselect menu.
Easy to use & understand, it gives you result & insight
It helps in keeping a track of my mood and being absolutely honest to myself without thinking of how anyone else would feel.

content: Helpful! I don't have any paid features so I really like how much I can do for free. It's a nice change from a lot of other "free" apps.

MindDoc is the best mental health tracker I've used. I used to use Daylio for a while, but I prefer MindDoc primarily for the 3x a day check-ins. What this app does of help you to be aware of your thoughts and your feelings. This helps me a lot to try and change my negative thinking pattern.

I never write reviews and I've never recorded my mood, but this is an excellent resource. Even though this is no substitute for therapy, it even gives you resources to try to help you understand what's going on and to me that's a lot of consideration put in for a free app. Thank you.
Good resource

The app is really useful for allowing yourself to just say everything on your mind and relieve yourself a bit. It is helpful in understanding different aspects of mental health. But the app has really helped me understand my problems and act on them very positively
replyContent: Dear Sarra, thank you for reaching out. We understand that the notifications might be stressful when having an intense time anyway. You might try to just turn them off or change the notification times to a more convenient time, when you feel like there might be a time coming up when they feel like a burden to you. We hope this helps. Your MD team

content: Love the app, just wish there was more free exercises or a monthly subscription option. Otherwise it has been helpful!

Great

content: This app has helped me express how I feel. I have been going through so much and this app has helped. I truly do recommand this if you are gping through hard times. This app also has a personal theripist you can talk to if you need help with anything.
Exceptionally good

You have to be consistent with answering the questions, and honest with yourself about how bad (or good!) things are. The insights are sometimes unhelpful, and sometimes right on the dot. I've also been trying Sanvello, but MindDoc gets more use from me because the 3x a day questions keep me coming back and being consistent. No ads, as far as I can tell.

Much later I discovered all the other options like meditations which are really nice with long breaks and for different topics, sleeping, fear, sadness. There are plenty. Also a lot of background information about depression and emotions and how to deal with them. I really really like this app and fully recommend it.

content: So lets give it the full initial 14 day, but I'll most likely return with a fifth star. I honestly think this is, not simply the best theropy app, but one of the greatest apps that I could have possible downloaded. Thanks for the genuine app-roach. *dunca

content: I love it :) it actually takes you answers into account and makes sure you get the right mental help if needed 10/10 would recommend.

The questions and the insights can get a little repetitive - especially if you've been treating your own issues for a while and have read all the self-help books out there - and it seems very easy to hit "critical emotional health", but it is a nice routine and it makes you think about how you're actually feeling at the time.
I think this app is very useful and helps keep me grounded mentally
This seems to be a good app. But not for me. I already know I am mentally I'll, so this is not the app for me.

replyContent: Dear Asha, We try to make as much core content as possible available for everyone but this is the best option we could think of to provide salary to our big and passionate team as well as to finance our research. content: nice
Free, questions take 2 mins, gives results that help and you can take courses.

I especially like that it doesn't try to shower me with positive thinking tropes; apps that do that don't help at all these days.
very nice insights

Still a great app, but the previous design was much, much better.
replyContent: Dear Kenzie, thank you for your kind words and your suggestion. It is always helpful to hear user opinions like yours. We will discuss the order of our emotion tags with the team! Best wishes, Your MindDoc team

content: I've struggled with depression for my entire adult life, but the past year has been especially hard on my mental health, for obvious reasons. MindDoc is easy to use and doesn't take much time out of your day, and I recommend it.

content: Leave me alone three times a day is too much. Perhaps twice a day morning and night okay

As a mental health app, you should know better than this.
replyContent: Hello, first of all we are sorry to hear that you experienced our question as offensive. We take reviews like yours very serious and will discuss how to reframe the question and be more attentive of the issues you have raised! Thank you for your support. - Your Moodpath Team

replyContent: Hey, we are sorry that this happened. We are currently working on an account system so switching phones will become easier. We can merge your accounts if you switch phone but it is vital that you write down your UIDs. Please contact us via feedback@moodpath.de so we can figure out if we can get the data back!- Your Moodpath Team

replyContent: Hello, we can understand your opinion and criticism. Our team would like to make Moodpath accessible to as many people as possible which is why we offer as much of the core content of the app as possible (i.e. screening for symptoms of depression followed by a sound evaluation, mood tracking, and some insights like weekly overviews) free of charge.

replyContent: Dear Christian, thank you for the review. We do not collect any personal data, and as this app is designed to give the user full authority of his/her data. This means we cannot give you any data because we do not "have" it in the settings you can choose to export the data and delete it. If you delete your data no trace is is left behind.

replyContent: Hello, thank you for your review. Our team would like to make Moodpath accessible to as many people as possible which is why we offer as much of the content of the app as possible (i.e. screening for symptoms of depression followed by a sound evaluation, mood tracking, and some insights like weekly overviews) free of charge. - Your Moodpath Team

After the questions I can leave a comment to kind of explain my thoughts. And the positive/negative word choices to just kind of put with my mood. And the biweekly analysis are helpful. LOVE IT ALL

Moodpath has been a daily diary for me for the past few months. It's a great app for tracking how you feel throughout the day and also helps you to look back on how much progress you've made. I recommend this app 100% and its definitely worth a try.

helps alot, easy to use, i love this.

Helps to track and log how you're feeling with many layers of detail: theres a general mood scale, some specific feelings you can choose from, and a place for comments. Every couple weeks, it also compiles your data to help you see trends.

The questions maybe need a bit of a refresh as after a couple of months they feel a bit stale. Other than that it is helpful to keep track of thoughts and emotions and I know I have found it if benefit. Another plus is it doesn't push the paid option in your face all the time like some other apps, which actually has made me want to pay for it now!
Its helped me think about how I feel rather than what's happening around me. I can control my emotions, but not all the other things!

I dont really know what to say about the app other than it helped me realise the state of my mental health. All it takes is 14 days for it to give you an idea to what it may be, you can even miss a few days. of course you have to be fully honest with the questions or you wont get an accurate answer. Edit: I wish that certain features were still free, like adding emotions, ect. It still is very helpful in maybe giving you an idea, but you cant be as in depth as you may want without paying.
So far so good . New to this application and love it. I like the daily prompts and checklists. It will be interesting to read the results when the 14 days are up. Great!

Great concept, and very helpful! I had to uninstall the app for a brief period to free up space on my phone. I exported the data, as that feature was available. When I reinstalled the app and skimmed through the introduction/first assessment, I discovered that I could not important my exported profile data back to my account. This made me lose a LOT of progress. Personally, I believe you should not add the ability to export data if it cannot be loaded from the cloud, its a shame. Needs work.

it's given me a outlet for a lot of emotions i had no idea how to work through and helped me learn to work live with some of them to the point its manageable. and it's a good virtual vent that no one else can see.

It helps me to write out what in feeling which helps sometimes but is hard to look back on and read . It helps to give tips or suggestions to suggest why you feel the way you do wich is great But sometimes the way it organizes stuff is a bit slow or doesn't record it

replyContent: Moodpath can help you understand how mood and depression symptoms change over time. To many people who suffer from depression it seems like every day is as bad as the last, but for most, there is quite a bit of change going on. Realting those changes to things that are happening in your life can be a first step towards getting better.

content: This app is super helpful, professional, and super easy. I can't tell you how many apps I've tried over the years, to help you track your mood or keep a journal. I've always sucked at sticking with that kind of thing. After a few days I'm done. But this is simple & easy. I started using this app to help track my moods and symptoms for my therapists after severe trauma. It tracks everything and writes a summary of your mental health for you let doctors, as well as giving you insights to improve.
Its a great app I've only a short time but I think over time it will help me improve my self and help me overcome my bad times.

I downloaded the app to help me keep track of my mental health because I have some diagnosed conditions I need to manage. While it sometimes points out obvious-to-me things like "oh hey you might have depression", the daily journal entries have really helped me figure out patterns in my moods. This has helped me develop much more effective coping strategies. The tracking alone makes it a good app; I haven't explored any of the premium features yet.
I had something to express my feelings to
This is a very helpful app and makes me think of how I am behaving and my mental health
It really helps if you have depression and anxiety it might even be good if you have panic attacks, it's absolutely perfect and I love it!! Overall amazing app. :)
Perfect

replyContent: Dear Ivana, we are sorry to hear that you experienced this. It is not at all what we intend to do. The questions are diagnostic and have scientifically been approved as indicators for depression and anxiety. Please do let us know what you'd change! We are always happy about feedback. Please contact us via feedback@moodpath.de. - Your Moodpath Team

content: It's really helpful. Thank you!
gives me insightful advice that I know I will use for a long time
More helpful than I thought it would have been.

There's a big difference between extreme sadness and just feeling a little blue that day. Great app overall!!
replyContent: Dear Kayley, thank you for your review. We are always looking for ways to improve Moodpath and adding the option to indicate one’s level of loneliness clearer is a very good idea, we could add it to the Moodgraphs and statistics. Please feel free to contact us with further ideas or questions: feedback@moodpath.de - Your Moodpath Team

really good :)

replyContent: Dear Nikita, we understand your critism. Our team would like to make Moodpath accessible to as many people as possible which is why we offer as much of the core content of the app as possible. However, the more complex the app becomes, the more important it is for many professional employees to further develop the product.We hope you understand.

I'm giving 3 stars because even though it helped me get my feelings out there, it stopped giving me results, it says it will take a long time and will notify me when results are ready, it has said this three times in a row with no notification, when I go and check it says I have no indicating signs of depression even though I have the same symptoms as when I started using it (since October).

I wish that these kinds of apps didn't have 'Premium' or 'Plus' because, at least for me, I can't afford or get easy access to any sort of therapist and there have been times when I've needed one. Mental health apps are supposed to help you and if you can't have access to all the services, they're not going to help you as much as they could/should. I really hate that it has a paywall of sorts. It would be so much better without one. I understand you need to pay people and profit but... Please...
Pretty good so far, kinda makes you feel nice just reflecting on your feelings

been using this over a week now and so far so good, kinda helps me to cope with depression and anxiety pretty well

I just can't afford it...

replyContent: Thank you for reaching out. We are concerned and would like to help. In a situation like this it can be good to speak to someone. Please contact us via feedback@moodpath.de so we can figure out together who you can talk to in your country.

content: Pretty good. At first couldn't figure out how to answer questions because you have to slide this little white (hard to see) tab upwards if you're in Calendar mode. I just wish there was more for if you answer questions and are in a crisis. Like a mode to help calm you in a panic attack or a chat for when you're really depressed.

I find the app to be a good way to track my moods. I have to remember to track the mood I have at the moment, not my mood overall, to be sure it is accurate. I wish this app had a more specific question about "thinking about death." Whose death? Mine? A loved one? The difference between anxiety about the possibility of other people's death and thinking about suicide is huge. Let's have some specificity please!

i need this app

replyContent: Dear Alyssa, thank you for the review. We have been in contact via Mail, hope the data transfer worked accordingly! Sorry to hear that you do not find it as appealing as before the update. In case you want to give us detailed feedback on what to include or change we are always happy about it. Wirte us via feedback@moodpath.de. -Your Moodpath Team

Pay money to become a lab rat. Moodpath pretends to be a mood tracker but is focused only on clinical depression. If you have multipolar depression or any other condition this app will give you poor results. They have virtually no support not even an email address you can contact. All data you enter is recorded for their research. This is a very pretty app that pretends to do a lot while giving you very little. If you know you're depressed why are you using an app to tell you the same?

content: Great for keeping in track of my moods and what caused them.

You get notifications for the morning questions but not afternoon or night and there's no setting to receive them. To me it's a big issue because I end up with big gaps.

I have to keep resetting the notifications because they will work for one day and then stop. Also, I don't understand why, allowing the app to use location, it needs to stay running on top of everything.

content: Moodpath is helpful, because I'm not alone all the time.

I feel like I should get to change the times on off days --but once the time is set , I can't change it.
replyContent: Hi! Thanks for your review. You can change the notification times manually. Please go to "Settings" and click on "Notifications". Next enable "Custom question reminders" and type in your preferred notification times. We hope this is helpful". Feel free to contact us anytime if you have more questions: feedback@moodpath.de. - Your Moodpath Team

Was great until I read privacy agreement. They give your data to Facebook for marketing purposes using your mobile device's ad ID. I took screenshots of my last ~2 years of data and just uninstalled the program.
replyContent:
Hello, we had a look at the transparency feature of Facebook. Facebook registered the Events: "App installed"
& "App opened" from the Moodpath-App. This was never our intention. Even though this was all within the legal limits, our team was always split and we decided against using Facebook SDK and deleting it from the App! Your Moodpath Team

replyContent:
Hello, we took a closer look at the transparency feature of Facebook. Facebook registered the Events: "App installed"
& "App opened" from the Moodpath-App. This was never our intention. Even though this was all within the legal limits, our team was always split and in the end we decided against using Facebook SDK and deleting it from the App.

replyContent: Hello, thank you for your review. On Android this happens occasionally as some of the phone manufactures kill the Apps in order to optimise battery usage. Please check https://dontkillmyapp.com for infos on how to disable this function on your phone! Hope this was helpful. -Your Moodpath Team

content: It really helps , filling it in and stracking how i feel , easy to use and understand

It has only been a few days but it feels nice to check in with myself in the morning, afternoon and evening. It's less demanding than having a journal. It allows me to be honest with myself. It is interesting to see how much my mood changes. So far I am enjoying it.
I would like to suggest this app to anyone looking for some understanding of their own behaviour pattern.

Extremely helpful. The idea of writing not about my day but my feelings give me time to think about myself and see how my emotions affect my daily routine. The only draw back that I see is that I cannnot log my mood/ journal in past. At times, I am busy and miss the notification but that doesn't change how I feel and thus the app should not stop me from writing about I felt in somepoint in time. It will be great to able to write about my feelings when I have time.
It really helps with calming down
Very straight forward and helpful in being more mindful about how and what you're feeling. The check-ins are quick and painless but still allow you to express my thoughts in my own words and add tags
Only just started using it but seems good so far
Trying it out
good

This has helped me so much. I have ptsd, depression, severe anxiety, & panic disorder. This helps me keep track of everything I am feeling & I can understand what causes my panic attacks, what causes flashbacks, & it helps me log what I talk about with my therapist as well because it operates like a journal. You can enter more than just 3 times a day when it asks you questions. It's amazing to see my emotional waves on a graph. I feel like I understand my own brain. No assessment yet, 4 days til

This app has helped me so much in my life. It enables me to keep track of my mental health without me having to put what I feel into words. The questions that you're asked not only help you track your state of mind and your place in life, but they also open your eyes to the way your mind and body are responding to your mental illness. It helps you realise when and why you need help. If you're feeling overwhelmed by your thoughts and emotions or feeling numb - get this app. Trust me. Just do it.

This app is very interesting. Whether or not the fortnightly diagnoses are right, the questions make you think about how your mood changes throughout the day. It also allows you to identify some long standing behaviours that may have become habit and you now think are normal but that don't have to be the norm.
Helps me to think
Just started using but seems quite helpful
Really helpful on giving me ideas about what I should pay attention to in my mental health. It offers sociological help and things to help you calm down. I really like it

This app is exceptional! I was wary about downloading it at first, because mood tracking apps aren't always what you expect, but Moodpath is great. I also was expecting pop- up ads, but nope; none. Moodpath can be used very well without paying a single coin. I highly recommend this app!
This app has helped me keep track of my mood consistently for over 3 months thus far! No other app has kept me consistently aware of my mood and mental health. I am so grateful!
I wish they would change up the questions instead of the same ones over and over other than that I really like this app
Its really helpful if you want to stat ur mood of the whole week
I love it. It's helping me a lot right now
